Reorder a factor (or character vector) by descending frequency

Source: R/factor_utils.R
mysterycall_reorder_by_freq.Rd

Returns a factor whose levels are ordered from most to least common. This is the standard preparation step before passing a categorical variable to ggplot2 bar charts or gtsummary::tbl_summary().

Usage

mysterycall_reorder_by_freq(x, decreasing = TRUE, na_level = NULL)

Arguments

x

A factor or character vector.

decreasing

Logical. TRUE (default) sorts most-frequent first; FALSE sorts least-frequent first.

na_level

Character scalar or NULL. When non-NULL, NA values are replaced with this string and included as the last level. Default NULL (NAs kept as NA).

Value

A factor with levels sorted by frequency.

Details

Equivalent to forcats::fct_infreq() but implemented in pure base R with no additional dependencies.

See also

Other provider characteristics: mysterycall_academic_patterns(), mysterycall_age_category(), mysterycall_assign_region(), mysterycall_check_academic_name_patterns(), mysterycall_classify_medical_school(), mysterycall_classify_practice_setting(), mysterycall_classify_ruca(), mysterycall_collapse_rare(), mysterycall_extract_physician_name(), mysterycall_genderize(), mysterycall_get_academic_indicators_summary(), mysterycall_government_patterns(), mysterycall_impute_age(), mysterycall_most_common_gender(), mysterycall_parse_certification_subspecialty(), mysterycall_physician_age(), mysterycall_recode_credentials(), mysterycall_reconcile_specialty()

Examples

x <- c("B", "A", "A", "C", "B", "B", "C", NA)
mysterycall_reorder_by_freq(x)
#> [1] B    A    A    C    B    B    C    <NA>
#> Levels: B A C
# levels: B (3), A (2), C (2), then by appearance for ties

# Useful in ggplot2:
# ggplot(df, aes(x = mysterycall_reorder_by_freq(specialty))) + geom_bar()
